skill.name: delon-chart
skill.description: Guides Angular developers in building dashboards with @delon/chart and G2Plot components for ng-alain applications.
skill.summary: Build reactive Angular dashboard charts with @delon/chart components.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution, network, and weak-cryptography patterns. Review found these are Markdown code fences, inline documentation references, package names, sample labels, and two documentation URLs, with no executable script, shell invocation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks reported as command execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and inline code spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced sections are documentation examples for Angular templates and @delon/chart options, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside Markdown examples or inline formatting. No shell command, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: G2Plot text reported as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated G2Plot and sample chart labels as weak cryptographic algorithm indicators. The context is charting library documentation and construction dashboard sample data, not encryption, hashing, or security code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines refer to G2Plot charting and the Chinese label for design work. There is no cryptographic primitive, key handling, or security-sensitive transformation.

low_findings.title: False positive: Documentation links reported as network behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are repository and documentation references for @delon/chart. They are plain Markdown links and do not perform network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the package information section. No code fetches these URLs or sends local data to external services.

content.value_statement: Angular teams need consistent dashboard charts without researching every @delon/chart component. This skill provides component patterns, reactive data examples, and integration guidance for ng-alain dashboards.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to use chart-card, g2-bar, g2-mini-bar, g2-pie, g2-radar, timeline, and trend components.
Provides Angular standalone component import examples for @delon/chart modules.
Demonstrates signal and computed patterns for reactive chart data.
Documents chart options such as height, padding, legend display, inner radius, subtitle, and total values.
Includes dashboard examples for task status, task type, weekly growth, and construction progress timelines.
Covers loading, empty-state, responsive-height, and data-size guidance for chart views.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install @delon/chart or modify project files by itself.
Examples target @delon/chart 20.1.0 and may need updates for other versions.
Sample labels and dashboard data are tailored to a construction progress application.
It does not validate live chart rendering or benchmark application performance.

use_cases.title: Create a project dashboard
use_cases.description: Build chart cards, bar charts, pie charts, and trend indicators for task progress summaries.

use_cases.title: Standardize chart data patterns
use_cases.description: Convert service data into signal-based chart inputs that update cleanly with Angular change detection.

use_cases.title: Review chart implementation quality
use_cases.description: Check dashboard code for loading states, empty states, responsive sizing, and excessive template logic.

prompt_templates.title: Add a basic chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-chart skill to add a simple @delon/chart bar chart to my Angular standalone component. Include imports, template usage, and signal-based data.

prompt_templates.title: Build a status dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-chart skill to design a dashboard section with chart cards, task status bars, task type pie charts, and trend indicators for my Angular app.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor chart data flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-chart skill to refactor my chart data from subscription-assigned arrays to computed signals. Preserve loading and empty states.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a chart-heavy page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-chart skill to review this Angular dashboard for @delon/chart best practices, performance risks, responsive sizing, and template complexity.

output_examples.input: I need a dashboard for task status and task type totals.
output_examples.output: A dashboard plan with chart cards for totals, a bar chart for status counts, a donut chart for type distribution, and signal-based computed data.

output_examples.input: My chart does not update when I push a new data point.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to replace direct array mutation with signal updates so Angular change detection receives a new array reference.

output_examples.input: Review my page for @delon/chart performance issues.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering computed transformations, fixed chart heights, lazy loading, loading states, empty states, and practical data point limits.

best_practices: Use computed signals to transform source data before passing it to chart components.
Define responsive but stable chart heights so layouts do not shift during rendering.
Provide loading and empty states for every chart that depends on asynchronous data.
anti_patterns: Do not mutate chart data arrays directly when using Angular signals.
Do not place complex filtering and mapping expressions inside Angular templates.
Do not render very large bar or pie datasets without limiting or aggregating data points.

faq.question: What Angular library does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers @delon/chart components built on G2Plot for ng-alain Angular applications.

faq.question: Can it generate complete Angular components?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides patterns for standalone component imports, templates, signals, computed data, and chart configuration.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents the expected @delon/chart package and shows how to use it after installation.

faq.question: Which chart types are included?
faq.answer: It includes chart-card, bar, mini bar, pie, radar, timeline, and trend examples.

faq.question: Is it only for construction dashboards?
faq.answer: No. The examples use construction progress data, but the patterns apply to many Angular dashboard domains.

faq.question: Does the skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. It contains documentation links only and does not include code that sends data to external services.
